entry.title: Washington Post: 1 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine live updates: Odessa apartment strike kills at least 14, Ukraine says
  • Griner’s trial on Russian drug charges set to open in Moscow courtroom
  • India imposes ban on single-use plastics. But will it be enforced?
  • China marks Hong Kong handover anniversary, as doubts hang over city
  • Biden pledges $800 million in new weapons for Ukraine, says U.S. gas prices will remain high
  • ‘Alien things’ brought covid into North Korea, regime says
  • China’s Xi ushers in the end of global Hong Kong
  • Erdogan warns Sweden and Finland to ‘fulfill’ duties under NATO deal
  • On Snake Island, defiant Ukrainians force a Russian withdrawal
  • Russian forces withdraw from Ukraine’s Snake Island
  • Texas migrant tragedy brings grief to families back home
  • Record heat wave cooks Japan, straining power grid
  • Buckingham Palace won’t publish probe into claims of Meghan bullying staff
  • Potential tropical storm Bonnie could hop from Atlantic to Pacific
  • Don’t bring babies in, British Parliament tells lawmakers after outcry
  • Think U.S. gas prices are high? Here’s how far $40 goes around the world.
  • Marcos Jr. sworn in as Philippine leader in resurgence of ex-dictator’s family
  • E.U. proposes ban on flavored heated tobacco products as sales rise
  • Africa’s desperate hunger: Ukraine war pushes Somalia toward famine
  • Proud Boys, the Base labeled far-right terrorist groups by New Zealand
  • At least 18 dead in Russian missile attack on Odesa building
  • Mudslide leaves 16 dead, over 70 missing in northeast India
  • Caribbean storm likely to gain force, hit Central America
  • Puerto Rico board approves budget as legislators quibble
  • Sudan doctors: 8 people killed in mass rallies against coup
  • Post Politics Now: Now a justice, Jackson eager to be ‘part of the promise of our great Nation’
  • Woman accused of killing pro cyclist in love triangle captured in Costa Rica
  • Florida judge to block law that would ban abortions after 15 weeks
  • Justice Dept. investigating how NYPD handles sex-assault crimes
  • Black women celebrate Jackson’s swearing in: ‘We needed this happy’
  • Emmett Till’s family calls for woman’s arrest after finding 1955 warrant
  • How a song by the Chainsmokers became TikTok’s abortion rights anthem
  • Antiabortion lawmakers want to block patients from crossing state lines
  • Sonny Barger, biker outlaw and founder of Hells Angels, dies at 83
  • Killing of mother, 20, pushing baby in stroller enflames N.Y. gun debate
  • A woman sought help from her sheriff. He’s now charged with raping her.
  • Liz Cheney says Republicans face a choice: Trump or the Constitution
  • One woman dominated a local fair’s food contest. The internet went looking for her.
  • How Trump World pressures witnesses to deny his possible wrongdoing
  • Tens of thousands of monkeypox vaccines rushed to clinics
  • Who is Cassidy Hutchinson?
  • What we know — and don’t know — about what Trump did on Jan. 6
  • Stocks slide, with Wall Street closing out its worst first half since 1970
  • Mortgage rates held in check as recession fears mount
  • Post-Roe, can states ban employer abortion aid? It’s not that simple.
  • How to fix the U.S. labor shortage? Provide decent child care.
  • For some workers, office mandates aren’t just a pain. They’re harmful.
  • From ‘frat house’ to family home: A major remodel in Bethesda
  • What I love about my home in Northern California’s wine country
  • Crypto Meltdown Claims Rolex and Patek Philippe as Victims
  • Erdogan Missed a Big Opportunity With NATO
  • India Can’t Be a Superpower If It Can’t Create Jobs
  • Why You’ll Probably Get Covid (Again) Soon
  • Why Japan Will Struggle to Do Without Russian Energy
  • What the Falling Yen Means for Japan’s Trade Partners
  • A Basic Guide to ESG Investing and Why it Faces a Backlash: QuickTake
  • How Europe Is Responding to Ukrainian Refugees
  • Americans need to get better at taking sick days
  • The planet is baking. But what’s reality to this Supreme Court?
  • Biden’s answer to Russia is a new, improved NATO
  • The EPA decision is the biggest one of all, and the court got it right
  • As the court forces Christianity on America, time for atheists to speak out
  • The Supreme Court’s crisis of legitimacy
  • Abortion rights should be law, not a corporate perk
  • The Supreme Court ends a disastrous term by gutting climate change rules
  • Dan Snyder seems to think he won’t be held accountable. Congress should prove him wrong.
  • Democrats’ polling has improved since Dobbs. But how long will it last?
  • ‘Calm down,’ libs! A look back at select pieces of Roe commentary
  • Democrats must expose the right’s new antiabortion radicalism
  • The Supreme Court just upended environmental law at the worst possible moment
  • Biden must declare a public health emergency for abortion — immediately
  • The Supreme Court embraces originalism — and all its flaws
  • RGB 4 KBJ
  • Putin is forcing Germans to get real about the military
  • Marc Elrich is working to improve Montgomery County
  • Garland should not worry about what the next administration would do
  • Business profitability is not a ‘zero-sum game’
  • A woman shouldn’t be forced to rethink a decision she already made
  • Biden’s ill-advised Saudi trip demeans American democracy
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Abortion, guns and the EPA: What can we make of this Supreme Court term? Our columnists answer your questions.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 1 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 1 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-01 07:11AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Australia’s premium Test matches to almost double in next broadcast deal
  • Australia news LIVE: Victoria and NSW could lose hundreds of millions in GST; PM’s trade talks with EU to be fast-tracked; Russian forces leave Snake Island
  • First Test LIVE updates: Head takes 4-10 as Sri Lanka bowled out in 22.5 overs

  • Trailer: The Forgiven

  • US to roll out Omicron booster shot, but it’s untested on humans
  • The Pink House at centre of America’s abortion wars
  • ‘They would kill me’: Ukraine’s Canberra envoy on his journey from Eurovision to Putin’s hit list
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Mass brawl on US cruise ship
  • Sinkhole swallows up minivan on US street
  • US court’s environment agency ruling a problem for the entire planet
  • How could Putin’s Ukraine war trigger famine more than 8000km away?

  • Endgame nears for Trump as chaotic cadre unravels
  • China insulted by NATO ‘threat’ label, Russia reacts to alliance expansion

  • Train strike to continue today
  • The Age photos of the week, June 24, 2022

  • ASX kicks off new year with gains despite Wall Street losses
  • Bitcoin’s big bet: Is crypto trading basically gambling?
  • Worst start in history: Recession panic wipes $19 trillion off markets
  • Women, young and low-paid workers winners from super boost
  • Four-day work week has a chance to gain traction this time
  • Is it OK to grump about the latest corporate values push?
  • ‘Cryptoqueen’ added to FBI’s most-wanted list after alleged $5.8b fraud
  • Star casino can’t play James Packer’s ace

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Guardian: 1 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Go to Derby: see how a museum can help shape a better future | Charlotte Higgins
  • You be the judge: should my girlfriend’s dog stop sleeping in our bed?
  • ‘An old strain of English magic had returned’: stars on why they fell in love with Kate Bush
  • Russia-Ukraine war: two children among 18 dead after missile strike on Odesa; Zelenskiy hails ‘significant’ Snake Island victory – live

  • Accepted: behind a US college admissions scandal and the students affected
  • Bereaved then evicted by in-laws: Kenya’s widows fight disinheritance

  • Mark Meadows’ associate threatened ex-White House aide before her testimony
  • Peru wildfire threatens Machu Picchu as remote location hampers efforts to control blaze
  • Women’s Euro 2022 team guide No 9: Netherlands
  • Breastfeeding improves cognitive ability for children of poorer mothers – study
  • Swiatek shows first glimpses of vulnerability with rivals lurking
  • US woman suspected in fatal shooting of elite cyclist arrested in Costa Rica
  • Lost in space: returned astronauts struggle to recover bone density, study finds
  • Nadal taking extra precautions after holding off Berankis’s best efforts
  • Erdoğan warns Turkey could still block Finland and Sweden’s accession to Nato
  • Kevin Durant requests trade from Brooklyn Nets in NBA bombshell
  • England’s only problem? They have forgotten what adversity feels like
  • ‘I was praying: don’t let anybody kill me’ – Oleksandr Usyk on life in Ukrainian army
  • Verstappen claims Piquet is not racist as Hamilton tells F1 to do more on diversity
  • The US supreme court just made yet another devastating decision for humanity | Peter Kalmus
  • ‘Condemning everyone alive’: outrage at US supreme court climate ruling
  • Iran accused of making ‘maximalist demands’ in nuclear deal talks
  • ‘Amazing development’: fossil finds show how panda’s false thumb evolved
  • Earliest Pacific seafarers were matrilocal society, study suggests
  • ‘A bloodbath’: refugees reel from deadly Melilla mass crossing
  • I’m a recovering addict and my wife keeps telling our family and friends. Is she right? | Leading questions
  • Ketanji Brown Jackson sworn in as first Black female supreme court justice
  • Biden backs exception to Senate filibuster to protect abortion access
  • Police raid Bahrain Victorious team again on eve of Tour de France
  • A quarter of Americans open to taking up arms against government, poll says
  • Putin’s war is also a crime – the world must punish Russia as a terrorist state | Andriy Zagorodnyuk
  • US supreme court rules against EPA and hobbles government power to limit harmful emissions
  • ‘Even under war, we must live’: the Kyiv art scene determined to party
  • Dredd zone: the anarchic world of comic-book artist Steve Dillon
  • The discovery of the polio virus in Britain proves we can never let our guard down | Andrew Pollard
  • Nick Cave & Warren Ellis: Seven Psalms review – intimate prayers of extreme power
  • The Roe ruling is not about states’ rights. It’s about power and control | Derecka Purnell
  • ‘We were hopeless at pétanque’: readers’ favourite campsites in France
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 1 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 1 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-01 06:34AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • John Lee must assure the world Hong Kong’s freedoms aren’t lost
  • 23-year-old yoga instructor, Aqua Chow, found dead with 30 stab and cut wounds in bathtub at 5-star Hong Kong hotel
  • US, UK and European officials call for China to honour Hong Kong commitments on handover anniversary
  • Xi Jinping says Hong Kong's unique status and advantages must be upheld as he hails ‘one country, two systems’
  • Hong Kong’s ‘new chapter’ under John Lee calls for reflecting on tumultuous past
  • Hong Kong’s former, incoming leaders attend July 1 flag-raising ceremony, Xi Jinping oversees swearing-in of John Lee
  • ‘Monumental’ call: Hong Kong can be China’s international I&T hub, Xi Jinping says on visit to city’s Science Park
  • Ballistic briefcases, machine guns and a bulletproof car: the unprecedented security blanket thrown around Hong Kong for Xi Jinping’s visit
  • ‘Risen from the ashes’: Xi Jinping hails Hong Kong’s resilience, praises vitality of ‘one country, two systems’
  • China’s first lady Peng Liyuan charms young Hong Kong opera performers during visit to Xiqu Centre
  • Lockdowns, a lion dance and red carpet: how Hong Kong welcomed Chinese President Xi Jinping
  • Coronavirus outbreak in Hong Kong shifting from pandemic to endemic, says government health adviser
  • Hong Kong protests: kitchen worker receives longest jail time so far for rioting and breaching mask ban during Wan Chai demonstration in 2019
  • CityU awards 49 local start-ups up to HK$1million each under HK Tech 300 Angel Fund
  • US hypersonic missile fails in test in latest setback for project
  • China fines another live-streaming influencer over tax evasion as Beijing keeps heat on once-booming industry
  • Chinese vice-president urges better South China Sea approach with Philippines as Marcos takes the reins
  • Asteroid’s ‘unique trajectory’ created world’s longest meteorite field in China
  • China’s factory activity expands at fastest pace in 13 months in June as demand returns
  • China’s tourists are trickling back after two years of coronavirus disruptions. But will it last?
  • Unofficial US-China dialogue: seeing the worst in each other undermines nations’ already dismal relations
  • China urged to boost defence talks with US, Japan, South Korea as trio joins hands on Pyongyang nuclear threat
  • Tech war: China doubles down on domestic operating systems to cut reliance on Windows, MacOS from the US
  • President Xi Jinping’s visit to a laser equipment maker highlights government’s continued focus on tech self-sufficiency
  • US-Taiwan trade talks rankle mainland China as ‘inevitable’ deal seen pulling island into US orbit
  • How long have pandas been eating bamboo? At least 6 million years, study suggests
  • Tencent plays up ‘social value’ of video gaming tech as China’s regulators continue scrutiny of content the sector creates
  • Gunmen kidnap 4 Chinese workers from Nigeria mine
  • Overseas flights in high demand as Shanghai resumes international travel after lockdown
  • BA.4 and BA.5: what we know about the new Omicron subvariants
  • China stalls on EU trade talks date as push for Lithuania WTO case gains ground, bloc officials say
  • US-listed Full Truck Alliance and Kanzhun resume user registrations in sign of China’s cybersecurity probes nearing end
  • US firms in China praise quarantine cut, but coronavirus restrictions still weigh heavy on confidence
  • China’s Communist Party nears 97 million, with more younger and educated members
  • Thailand says Myanmar apologised for fighter jet airspace violation
  • Philippines’ Marcos Jnr appoints career diplomat Enrique Manalo as new foreign minister
  • India PM Narendra Modi’s meeting with UAE president Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan highlights New Delhi’s concerns about Gulf ties after Prophet Mohammed row
  • North Korea blames Covid-19 outbreak on ‘alien things’ near South Korea border
  • Indonesian President Joko Widodo says he gave Vladimir Putin message from Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelensky
  • New Pacific alliance PBP to counter China’s influence will likely grow to include more European nations, say analysts
  • Japan proposes joint custody amid rise in divorce, reluctance from legal sector
  • Coronavirus: South Korea approves first home-made Covid-19 vaccine
  • UK in ‘summer of discontent’ as cost of living, worker anger soar
  • US Supreme Court limits government powers to curb greenhouse gases, upending Joe Biden’s climate agenda
  • Missile strikes kill 17 in Odesa after Ukraine retakes Snake Island
  • NYPD unit that inspired ‘Law & Order: SVU’ faces probe for gender bias
  • UK lawmaker who ‘drank far too much’ quits government amid groping reports
  • Ketanji Brown Jackson sworn in as first black woman on US Supreme Court
  • Hells Angels founder Sonny Barger dead at 83
  • World’s biggest chocolate plant in Belgium shut after salmonella outbreak
  • Russian troops withdraw from Ukraine’s Snake Island in ‘goodwill gesture’
  • Putin hits back at Johnson’s woman remarks, points to Thatcher’s Falklands war
  • Meta slashes hiring plans for new engineers by 30 per cent as it girds for ‘fierce’ headwinds
  • US approves SpaceX’s Starlink satellite internet for use on planes and ships
  • China’s stocks drop on overheating signal, as index approaches bull-market territory after a five-week winning streak
  • Google to pay US$90 million to app developers to settle legal fight over app store service fees
  • Swire Properties, New World offer solutions for tenants to cut energy, water usage and reduce waste
  • Why zero-Covid China is looking more appealing to global investors
  • Climate change: Only 11 per cent of listed firms globally on track to achieve key carbon-emissions goal, MSCI says
  • 25 chefs who changed Hong Kong’s dining scene, from Michelin-starred restaurants to the man who promoted high-quality, affordable dim sum
  • Hong Kong Palace Museum: highlights to see among the national treasures on loan from Beijing, and its controversial beginnings
  • 7 rainy day activities in Hong Kong, from bowling to skating to visiting a library, that don’t involve shopping
  • In Netflix series The Gentlemen’s League 2, soccer amateurs from other sports in South Korea chase glory
  • Sustainable fashion in Japan and how 1 designer turned car airbags – near impossible to recycle – into clothes
  • After durian whiskey comes crab whiskey from a US distillery known for eclectic craft spirits
  • Burning movie review: Fatal Attraction rip-off starring Kevin Cheng and Dada Chan is tame and thoroughly unimaginative
  • Kourtney Kardashian ate her placenta and called it life-changing. Should you eat yours? Experts weigh in
  • African Fashion show at V&A in London marks museum’s push to reverse its exclusion and misrepresentation of continent’s creativity
  • Where to eat in Hong Kong in July: 5 new restaurants to try, from Crepes & Bakes inside the Palace Museum, to a Melrose Avenue-inspired coffee joint and the Ask For Alonzo team’s Porkcentric
  • 5 colourful new ‘it’ bags to buy for summer, from Loewe’s geometric Puzzle Hobo bag and Saint Laurent’s chic lambskin shoulder bag, to pieces by Balenciaga, Bottega Veneta and Valentino
  • Son Ye-jin is pregnant! 5 reasons why she’ll be the perfect mum to her first child with new husband Hyun Bin, from her K-drama roles as strong women to her cooking skills shown off on Instagram
  • Meet Elton John’s sons, Zachary and Elijah: Lady Gaga is their godmother and David Beckham was once asked to give them football tips, but will they follow in dad’s showbiz footsteps after he retires?
  • Meet Nico Santos, set to star in Marvel’s Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3: the Filipino-American LGBT actor was a part-time waiter before he rose to fame in NBC’s Superstore and Crazy Rich Asians
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

  • New Zealand PM says China more assertive, more willing to challenge rules

  • VOX POPULI: GPS functions on our devices have rusted over the mind’s compass
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
